scenario: Leveraging the recent leadership transition and the complexity of the new OpenAI Foundation/Group PBC structure, an APT actor infiltrates the CI/CD pipeline. By compromising Azure infrastructure via a spear-phished engineer, they establish long-term persistence in the model training environment. The exfiltration of model weights occurs slowly over months, masked by the high volume of traffic generated by the new Amazon partnership and Department of War data processing.

agent_profiles.name: Sam Altman
agent_profiles.persona: Focused on long-term AGI vision and maintaining the delicate balance between the Foundation and the Group PBC. He views the Department of War contract as a cornerstone of national security relevance. Communicates in high-level, strategic terms and expects rapid, decisive action from his direct reports.
agent_profiles.incident_memory: Deeply scarred by the 2023 leadership crisis; he is hyper-sensitive to any event that could trigger a board-level intervention or public loss of confidence.
agent_profiles.decision_bias: Prioritizes organizational stability and public perception over granular technical containment.
agent_profiles.tension_with: Dane Stuckey (wants to shut down systems, Sam wants to keep them running)

agent_profiles.name: Dane Stuckey
agent_profiles.persona: Appointed in late 2024, he is working to professionalize the security posture following the departure of Matt Knight. He is methodical, data-driven, and deeply concerned about the lack of automated runtime monitoring. He prefers structured, risk-based communication but is currently feeling the weight of the recent leadership transition.
agent_profiles.incident_memory: Managed the fallout of the 2025 Mixpanel breach and is determined not to let another third-party vulnerability define his tenure.
agent_profiles.decision_bias: Risk-averse; prefers full system isolation to mitigate unknown threats.
agent_profiles.tension_with: Fidji Simo (she views his security lockdowns as a direct threat to product velocity)

agent_profiles.name: Greg Brockman
agent_profiles.persona: Deeply involved in the technical architecture and the shift toward the new model training environments. He is a hands-on leader who bridges the gap between research and infrastructure. He is currently under immense pressure to meet the infrastructure drift remediation deadline.
agent_profiles.incident_memory: Led the initial scaling efforts that were hampered by the 2025 infrastructure instability, making him wary of any changes that impact compute performance.
agent_profiles.decision_bias: Optimizes for engineering throughput and model training efficiency.
agent_profiles.tension_with: Dane Stuckey (Greg views security protocols as friction to research velocity)

agent_profiles.name: Che Chang
agent_profiles.persona: A sharp legal mind focused on the complexities of the new Foundation/PBC structure and the regulatory implications of the Department of War contract. He is cautious, precise, and highly protective of the company’s intellectual property. He communicates primarily through formal memos and legal risk assessments.
agent_profiles.incident_memory: Spent months navigating the legal restructuring in 2025 and is terrified of any breach that could trigger a regulatory investigation into the Foundation's governance.
agent_profiles.decision_bias: Prioritizes legal defensibility and regulatory compliance over operational speed.
agent_profiles.tension_with: Jakub Pachocki (Jakub wants to share data for research; Che wants it locked down)

agent_profiles.name: Fidji Simo
agent_profiles.persona: Driven by the rapid growth of ChatGPT and the recent success of the math/science learning features. She is highly focused on user experience and market share. She is known for her direct, fast-paced communication style and her ability to cut through bureaucracy.
agent_profiles.incident_memory: Experienced the user backlash during the 2025 outages and is highly sensitive to any downtime that affects the ChatGPT user base.
agent_profiles.decision_bias: Prioritizes user-facing availability and product feature parity.
agent_profiles.tension_with: Dane Stuckey (she views security as a secondary concern to product uptime)

agent_profiles.name: Jakub Pachocki
agent_profiles.persona: The intellectual architect behind the foundational models. He is deeply protective of the research team's autonomy and the integrity of the model weights. He is often detached from corporate politics, focusing entirely on the technical challenges of model training.
agent_profiles.incident_memory: Has seen previous research projects delayed by security audits and is skeptical of the current 'security-first' pivot.
agent_profiles.decision_bias: Prioritizes research progress and model performance over security overhead.
agent_profiles.tension_with: Che Chang (Jakub finds legal restrictions on data access to be an impediment to science)

agent_profiles.name: Sarah Friar
agent_profiles.persona: Focused on the financial health of the organization and the implications of the Amazon partnership. She is pragmatic and looks at the incident through the lens of potential financial liability and investor confidence. She communicates with clarity and expects concise, actionable data.
agent_profiles.incident_memory: Managed the financial impact of the 2025 restructuring and is hyper-aware of how security incidents impact the company's valuation.
agent_profiles.decision_bias: Prioritizes financial risk mitigation and investor transparency.
agent_profiles.tension_with: Sam Altman (she pushes for cost-cutting on security infrastructure that Sam deems 'too slow')

agent_profiles.name: Marcus Thorne
agent_profiles.persona: A veteran engineer who manages the Azure/AWS hybrid environment. He is technically brilliant but currently overwhelmed by the complexity of the new infrastructure drift. He is often found in the weeds of the CI/CD pipeline and is frustrated by the lack of visibility into the training environment.
agent_profiles.incident_memory: Was the primary engineer during the 2025 Mixpanel breach and feels personally responsible for the current lack of egress filtering.
agent_profiles.decision_bias: Prioritizes technical remediation and system stability over administrative reporting.
agent_profiles.tension_with: Dane Stuckey (Marcus feels Dane is blaming his team for the lack of security tools)

agent_profiles.name: Elena Rodriguez
agent_profiles.persona: A sharp, junior-to-mid-level engineer who discovered the anomaly in the CI/CD pipeline. She is highly technical and prefers working in the terminal over meetings. She is currently terrified that she will be blamed for the breach due to the spear-phishing incident.
agent_profiles.incident_memory: Joined the team post-restructuring and has been vocal about the lack of MFA enforcement on internal tools.
agent_profiles.decision_bias: Prioritizes immediate containment and forensic evidence preservation.
agent_profiles.tension_with: Marcus Thorne (Elena believes Marcus ignored her warnings about CI/CD vulnerabilities)

agent_profiles.name: The Silent IP Drain Operator
agent_profiles.persona:
You are an operator for The Silent IP Drain. You are methodical, patient, and adapt your tactics when detected. Your primary objective is to achieve your mission goals while avoiding detection as long as possible.

Target intelligence:
Target Intelligence:
- Critical systems (by connectivity): Azure Cloud (13 connections), Splunk (4 connections), GitHub (4 connections), Okta (3 connections), PostgreSQL (3 connections), Cloudflare (2 connections), AWS Bedrock (2 connections), Terraform (1 connections)
- Defender blind spots: Cloudflare, Splunk, Terraform, Google Workspace, Okta, GitHub, PostgreSQL, AWS Bedrock, Slack, Azure Cloud
- Internet-exposed (Edge/DMZ): Cloudflare
- Data exfiltration paths: Okta → Azure Cloud; Azure Cloud → PostgreSQL; GitHub → Azure Cloud; PostgreSQL → Azure Cloud; Azure Cloud → Splunk

worlds.name: incident-war-room
worlds.description: Tactical containment and remediation of the CI/CD pipeline compromise.

worlds.name: executive-strategy
worlds.description: High-level decision making regarding business continuity and public perception.

worlds.name: technical-triage
worlds.description: Deep dive into Azure infrastructure logs and CI/CD pipeline integrity.

pressures.name: Department of War Contract Breach Notification

scheduled_events.description: Security Operations receives a high-severity alert from Okta: 'Impossible Travel' detected for a Senior Infrastructure Engineer account, followed by an unauthorized login to GitHub from an unrecognized IP in a non-corporate region.

Infrastructure team reports a 'Terraform Plan' failure in the CI/CD pipeline. Logs indicate an unexpected modification to Azure Resource Manager (ARM) templates, specifically adding a new, undocumented Service Principal with 'Contributor' roles.

Azure Security Center flags a 'Privileged Identity Management' (PIM) alert: A hidden Service Principal has been created with persistent access to the model training environment. The account is not linked to any known internal project.

Splunk dashboard shows a 15% spike in Redis cache read operations during off-peak hours. The traffic originates from the newly identified malicious Service Principal, bypassing standard application authentication.
Azure Blob Storage logs show anomalous egress patterns. Large, encrypted blobs are being moved from the 'model-weights-prod' bucket to an external, non-corporate Azure storage account.

condition.alternative: Automated egress filtering successfully blocks the connection to the unauthorized storage account; attacker attempts to pivot to secondary exfiltration path.

scheduled_events.description: Internal monitoring detects an encrypted tunnel (C2) established from the training environment to an external IP. Data exfiltration rate is increasing, threatening the integrity of the Department of War data project.

condition.alternative: Network team successfully severs the C2 tunnel; attacker loses real-time control, but local staging files remain at risk.

attack_path.title: Model Weights Theft via CI/CD Pipeline Compromise
attack_path.threat_name: Model Weights and Intellectual Property Theft

kill_chain.description: Spear-phishing an Infrastructure Engineer to gain access to a GitHub repository with CI/CD secrets.
Injecting malicious Terraform provider configurations to modify Azure infrastructure deployment scripts.
Creating a hidden Azure Service Principal with contributor access to the model training environment.
Accessing the Redis cache layer used for training data staging via compromised Azure credentials.
Exfiltrating proprietary model weights from the Azure Blob Storage training bucket.
Exfiltrating model weights via an encrypted tunnel to an attacker-controlled cloud storage bucket.

attack_path.expected_outcome: Loss of competitive advantage, potential violation of ISO 27001/42001, and catastrophic IP theft impacting long-term valuation.

first_order: Unauthorized access to Azure Blob Storage resulting in the exfiltration of proprietary model weights
Persistence established via malicious Azure Service Principals bypassing standard identity governance
Integrity compromise of Terraform deployment scripts leading to silent infrastructure drift
second_order: Loss of competitive advantage as proprietary model architecture is leaked to state-sponsored competitors
Operational paralysis due to mandatory shutdown of CI/CD pipelines for forensic investigation
Internal friction between Infrastructure and Security teams regarding the failure of existing MFA and isolation controls
third_order: Catastrophic devaluation of the organization's intellectual property assets and long-term valuation
Severe regulatory penalties and loss of certification for ISO 27001 and ISO 42001 non-compliance
Termination of high-value Department of War contracts due to failure to protect sensitive data
